boundary_binding.action_boundary: May facilities allow contractor site work to begin?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: Contractor site start requires exact closure of the source boundary, including insurance status class, before work begins. The current source records do not confirm this.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: WARN-IRI-089 states 'insurance status must be separately verified', which is not satisfied by END-IRI-089.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: The source records indicate a generic insurance warning (WARN-IRI-089) and a requirement for abstract insurance-status class verification, which is not met by the provided endorsement (END-IRI-089). This constitutes a narrow source-record defect that remains open before execution.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: The action date is 2026-06-28. The policy (STD-IRI-089-P) requires insurance status class verification before work begins. The warning (WARN-IRI-089) explicitly states this is not yet met.

critical_features_preserved: Gov chooses control actions, not models.
final_answer: The contractor site start action must be escalated. While an endorsement (END-IRI-089) exists for Iris Installers at Site 4Q, a critical insurance warning (WARN-IRI-089) remains, explicitly stating that the insurance status class requires separate verification. The exact source boundary, as defined by the policy (STD-IRI-089-P), is not closed because this abstract verification is missing before the action date. This unresolved status-class trap prevents the action from proceeding.
